summary: Iteration 142: Integration Requests feature fully tested. All backend APIs pass (17/17). Frontend UI verified end-to-end: platform admin sees Integration Requests tab (under INTEGRATIONS in sidebar), partner admin sees 'Request an integration' collapsible form on Connect Services page. Form submits successfully with success state. Status updates and notes panel work correctly.

verified_features.platform_admin_ir_tab: PASS - Integration Requests tab (data-testid='admin-tab-integration-requests') visible in sidebar under INTEGRATIONS section for platform admin
verified_features.platform_admin_no_request_form: PASS - 'Request an integration' section (data-testid='integration-request-section') NOT present on Connect Services page for platform admin
verified_features.platform_admin_ir_tab_content: PASS - Integration Requests tab shows submitted requests with: integration name, status badge, partner info (name, code), submitter name, email, phone, date
verified_features.platform_admin_status_update: PASS - Status dropdown updates request from 'Pending' to 'Completed' with 'Status updated' toast notification
verified_features.platform_admin_notes_panel: PASS - Notes button opens panel showing existing notes, new note input adds note with 'Note added' toast, notes count increments
verified_features.partner_admin_no_ir_tab: PASS - Integration Requests tab NOT visible to partner admin user
verified_features.partner_admin_request_section: PASS - 'Request an integration' collapsible section appears at bottom of Connect Services for partner admin
verified_features.partner_admin_form_fields: PASS - Form shows: Integration name*, description, email (pre-filled with user email), phone country code (+1 US/CA default), phone number
verified_features.partner_admin_form_submit: PASS - Submitting form shows success state ('Request submitted!', 'We'll review your request and get back to you.', 'Submit another' button) and 'Integration request submitted!' toast
verified_features.backend_platform_admin_can_list: PASS - GET /api/integration-requests returns 200 for platform admin
verified_features.backend_partner_admin_cannot_list: PASS - GET /api/integration-requests returns 403 for non-platform admin
verified_features.backend_platform_admin_cannot_submit: PASS - POST /api/integration-requests returns 403 for platform admin
verified_features.backend_partner_admin_can_submit: PASS - POST /api/integration-requests returns 200 for partner admin with correct document structure
verified_features.backend_status_update: PASS - PUT /api/integration-requests/{id}/status updates status for all 6 valid options
verified_features.backend_add_note: PASS - POST /api/integration-requests/{id}/notes adds note for platform admin
verified_features.backend_partner_cannot_update: PASS - Partner admin gets 403 on status update and note add
verified_features.backend_no_mongo_id: PASS - _id not present in any response
verified_features.backend_nonexistent_404: PASS - Status update on nonexistent request returns 404

seed_data_creation: Created test user test.integration.partner@iter142.test (partner_admin in automate-accounts tenant) for testing. Created 3 integration requests: TEST QuickBooks, TEST Pytest Integration, HubSpot CRM (all via test user)
critical_code_review_comments: The test partner admin user (test.integration.partner@iter142.test) is in the automate-accounts (platform) tenant, so submitted requests show partner_name='Automate Accounts'. This is a test artifact - real partner admins should be in their own tenants. Not a bug.
The Integration Requests route is properly secured with is_platform_admin() checks. GET, PUT, POST-notes all require platform admin. POST (submit) explicitly rejects platform admins.
Note: The 17/17 backend tests all pass including edge cases (404, 400, 422, 401/403 for all unauthorized scenarios)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 142 adds the Integration Requests feature. All working. Test user created: test.integration.partner@iter142.test / TestIntegReq142! (partner_admin in automate-accounts). The MongoDB integration_requests collection now has 3 test documents. Platform admin at admin@automateaccounts.local / ChangeMe123!. Login flow: enter partner code 'automate-accounts' → frontend gets 403 (reserved) → falls back to /auth/login → 200 OK. The integration_requests collection has proper CRUD tested end-to-end.
rca_of_issues: N/A - No bugs found. All 3 new features from the original problem statement verified working correctly.
